section: Chapter 1: The Ever-Evolving World of Science

questions.question: What is science?
questions.correct_answer: Systematic study of nature through observation and experimentation
questions.explanation: Science helps us understand natural phenomena and develop theories.

questions.question: Why is observation important in science?
questions.correct_answer: It is the first step in forming questions and hypotheses
questions.explanation: Careful observation allows scientists to notice patterns and problems that need explanation.

questions.question: What is a hypothesis?
questions.correct_answer: An educated guess to explain an observation
questions.explanation: A hypothesis can be tested through controlled experiments.

questions.question: What are the main steps of the scientific method?
questions.correct_answer: Observation, question, hypothesis, experiment, data analysis, conclusion
questions.explanation: Following these steps ensures systematic and reliable investigation.

questions.question: Name the three main branches of science.
questions.explanation: Physics studies matter and energy, Chemistry studies substances, Biology studies living organisms.

questions.question: How are science and technology related?
questions.correct_answer: Science provides knowledge, and technology applies it for practical purposes
questions.explanation: Technological tools can help scientists make new discoveries, and discoveries can lead to new technologies.

questions.question: What is the difference between a discovery and an innovation?
questions.correct_answer: Discovery is finding new knowledge; innovation is applying it practically
questions.explanation: For example, discovering electricity is a discovery, using it to create bulbs is an innovation.

questions.question: Why is evidence important in science?
questions.correct_answer: It ensures conclusions are reliable and reproducible
questions.explanation: Scientific conclusions are based on observed and measured data to avoid errors or bias.

questions.question: Why does scientific knowledge change over time?
questions.correct_answer: Because new discoveries and better techniques can modify or replace earlier theories
questions.explanation: Science is dynamic; learning never stops as evidence accumulates.

questions.question: Why is safety important in science experiments?
questions.correct_answer: To prevent accidents and injuries while performing experiments
questions.explanation: Using protective gear, handling chemicals properly, and following lab rules are essential.

notes.title: Science
notes.content: Systematic study of nature through observation and experimentation.

notes.title: Hypothesis
notes.content: An educated guess that can be tested experimentally.

notes.title: Scientific Method
notes.content: Observation → Question → Hypothesis → Experiment → Data Analysis → Conclusion.

notes.title: Branches of Science
notes.content: Physics: matter and energy; Chemistry: substances and reactions; Biology: living organisms.

notes.title: Science vs Technology
notes.content: Science provides knowledge; technology applies it practically.

facts.fact: Observation is the first step in the scientific method.
Hypotheses are tested using controlled experiments.
Scientific knowledge evolves as new evidence emerges.
Innovations are practical applications of scientific discoveries.
Safety in experiments prevents accidents and ensures proper handling of materials.
